data.title: Canada's Carney pledges to boost defense spending, support local industry if elected
data.description:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney vowed to ramp up the country’s military spending above the NATO target of 2% of GDP within a few years, while overhauling the country’s defense procurement process to reduce reliance on U.S. contractors and strengthen domestic industry.

Speaking at the Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) aeronautics plant in Montreal on Monday, Carney said the country must adapt to an “increasingly dangerous and divided world” by significantly reinvesting in its armed forces, according to multiple press reports.

He emphasized that while military spending will rise, it can also serve as an engine of industrial growth and job creation across Canada.

“It’s necessary for our safety,” he said, “but it can be done in a way that elevates our own industries and creates good jobs here in Montreal and across the country.”

He pledged to spend more on the military while supporting Canadian manufacturers, particularly in steel, aluminum and critical minerals.

Currently, Canada spends about 1.3% to 1.4% of GDP on defense, falling short of the NATO minimum commitment of 2%.

Carney, a former central banker of England and Canada, last month became prime minister after succeeding Justin Trudeau as leader of the Liberal Party. Carney called for a federal election to take place on April 28, pitting him mainly against the Conservatives led by Pierre Poilievre.

Canada will reach and exceed 2% defense spending threshold faster than previously planned, Carney pledged. Trudeau had targeted 2032 to meet the goal, but Carney said his timeline is “within a few years.”

To help manage this expanded military investment, Carney proposed the creation of a centralized defense procurement agency. This new body would consolidate expertise from across the government and streamline military purchases, with a clear directive to prioritize Canadian suppliers whenever possible.

Looking to European allies

It would also seek to expand security and trade relationships with European allies in light of what Carney described as increasingly adversarial behavior from the current U.S. administration.

“Our military has been both underfunded and overly reliant on the United States,” he said.

As part of his broader defense platform, Carney outlined plans to modernize Canada’s military with investments in submarines, icebreakers and potentially new fighter jets. He said the existing deal to purchase F-35s from Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) will be reviewed, with the option of sourcing aircraft from European partners instead.

In addition to procurement reform, Carney promised to raise the pay for members of the Canadian Armed Forces and support domestic defense firms in growing their export presence — particularly through free trade agreements Canada holds with Europe and Asia.

The Liberal leader also said his government would reassess federal business spending and shift investments toward defense, clean energy, housing and trade infrastructure.

Carney’s campaign promises aim to close the gap with allies who have criticized Canada’s underperformance on defense spending, while positioning national security as a catalyst for economic development at home.

data.title: Chinese gold ETF inflows so far in April exceed entire Q1 total, WGC says
data.description:
Gold futures edged lower on Monday but remained above $3,200/oz, as the market's risk appetite improved after President Trump exempted smartphones, computers and other electronic items from steep tariffs on China, at least temporarily.

"Perhaps some relief on the tariff front, with the exemption of some electronics maybe taking some of the safe haven bid out, [but] ongoing uncertainty about trade and tariffs, weakness in the dollar and softer yields tend to be supportive for gold," Zaner Metals senior metals strategist Peter Grant said.

Gold's one-day decline is not likely to become a short-term trend, Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities wrote, as "tariff headlines continue to hit the tape, the dollar continues to slide, and the Chinese continue to lurk in the shadows while holding the second most amount of U.S. debt on the planet... That should allow gold to attract enough safe haven flow to at least consolidate within striking distance of the all-time record high."

Gold has been supported by a jump in ETF inflows, a weaker dollar, and safe-haven appeal, but a sharp selloff in the long end of the U.S. Treasury curve also likely has triggered additional gold purchases, SP Angel analysts said, noting gold and Treasurys usually compete for safe-haven status, and investors may be reducing Treasury positions in favor of gold given concerns over the U.S. economy.

Goldman Sachs doubled down on its bullish stance on gold, raising its year-end forecast to $3,700/oz, citing stronger than expected central bank demand and heightened recession risks affecting ETF inflows.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed -0.5% to $3,204.80/oz, snapping a four-session winning streak but still the second-highest ever close for the front-month contract, while front-month April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.8% to $32.092/oz, its sixth straight daily gain.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Investment flows into Chinese physically backed gold ETFs far this month have surpassed flows for the entire Q1 and overtaken inflows registered by U.S.-listed funds, the World Gold Council reported.

Gold ETFs in China increased by 29.1 metric tons in the first 11 days of April, compared with inflows of 23.5 tons during the January-to-March period, WGC senior market strategist John Reade said.

"If the first quarter of this year was dominated by the U.S. tariff-related gold flows and Western ETF buying, the second quarter may have a very different theme, that of a surge in investor interest in gold from China," Reade wrote.

data.title: Thinkific Labs to convert all multiple voting shares to single voting common shares
data.description:
• Thinkific Labs (TSX:THNC:CA) announced on Monday that it will eliminate its dual-class structure by converting all 44,401,620 issued and outstanding multiple voting shares into subordinate voting shares on a one for one basis.
  • Upon completion of this transaction, the company will have 68,027,927 common shares issued and outstanding. With this change, all shareholders will have an equal vote per share, said the company. 
  • Upon approval of the Toronto Stock Exchange, the company anticipates that the common shares will begin trading on the TSX under the same symbol on or about April 21, 2025. 
  • Registered shareholders who hold certificates or DRS (Direct Registration System) advices representing the subordinate voting shares do not need to take any further action, said the company. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bessent eyes fall interviews for next Federal Reserve chair - report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Monday he will launch interviews sometime this fall to select the next Federal Reserve chair.

Current Fed boss Jerome Powell's term is set to end May 15, 2026.

Speaking in a wide-ranging interview with Bloomberg Television, Bessent also noted that the U.S. Treasury Department has enough room to boost buybacks -- "if we wanted" -- should the bond market show further signs of stress. 

Last week, the Treasury chief described last week's bond-market swoon as "uncomfortable but normal deleveraging."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Cue biopharma announces public offering
data.description:
• Cue Biopharma (NASDAQ:CUE) said on Monday that it is commencing an underwritten public offering of shares of its common stock and accompanying warrants to purchase shares of common stock. 
  • Shares of CUE slipped -12.76% to $0.69 during extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rocket Lab selected for multi-billion dollar U.S. and U.K. defense contracts
data.description:
• Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) on Monday said it has been selected to provide hypersonic test launch capability with its HASTE launch vehicle, engineering expertise, and other services through its participation in two multi-billion dollar government development programs for the United States and the United Kingdom.
  • Rocket Lab has been selected by the U.S. Air Force to participate within its Enterprise-Wide Agile Acquisition Contract (EWAAC), a $46 billion indefinite delivery-indefinite quantity (IDIQ) contract designed for the rapid acquisition of innovative technologies, engineering services, and technical solutions that develop the Air Force’s new capabilities.
  • The program has a contracting period through to 2031.
  • Further, Rocket Lab has also been selected by the United Kingdom’s Ministry of Defence (UK MOD) for its Hypersonic Technologies & Capability Development Framework (HTCDF), a ~$1.3 billion (£1 billion) framework to rapidly develop advanced hypersonic capabilities for the United Kingdom.
  • RKLB +5.43% after hours to $20.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Capital Power to buy two PJM generation assets in $2.2B deal, plans $500M share offering
data.description:
Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) said post-market Monday it agreed to acquire two gas-fired power plants in the U.S. for a combined US$2.2B from LS Power Equity Advisors, establishing a position in PJM Interconnection, the largest and most liquid North American power market.

The acquisition comprises a 100% interest in the 1,124 MW Hummel Station combined-cycle natural gas facility in Shamokin Dam, Pennsylvania, and 100% of the 1,023 MW Rolling Hills combustion turbine natural gas facility in Wilkesville, Ohio.

Following the deal closing, Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) said it will be positioned as one of five North American independent power producers with more than 10 GW of natural gas capacity, and the acquisition is consistent with its strategy to acquire flexible generation assets and its growth focus in the U.S. and PJM market.

Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) expects the acquisition to generate ~$443M in average annual adjusted EBITDA for the 2026-30 period and to average 17%-19% accretive to adjusted funds from operations per share.

The company said a concurrent $500M common share offering will fully address the equity funding requirement for the acquisition.
